title: Nostra Cross-Domain Adversarial Extension Ontology
description: A deliberately adversarial but valid additive ontology used to prove that cross-domain Space-local terms can extend core graph semantics without redefining them.

classes.description: Sovereign container for policies, contributions, and knowledge bundles.
Governed unit of knowledge, work, or evidence that can be linked and versioned.
Typed link between two governed graph subjects.
A declared ability or feature exposed by a Space or contribution surface.
Named graph scope used to partition evidence and traversal.
A cross-space governed finding meant to survive research-to-operations handoff.
An operational context record that can receive additive findings.

relations.description: A Contribution relates to another Contribution.
A Contribution is scoped by a ProvenanceScope.
A portable finding is promoted into an operational context without redefining either side's core semantics.
A portable finding may be validated against a core contribution.

properties.description: Canonical portable identifier for a graph subject or relation.
Authority ceiling for a bundled artifact or query.
Optional scope-specific reference required operationally for actor and agent scopes.
A small additive state label for portable cross-space handoffs.

extension_rules: Adversarial extension terms must remain namespace-prefixed under bridge: and additive to the core ontology.
Cross-domain additive terms may target core terms, but must not redefine their semantics.
The closed provenance-scope core set remains system, actor, and agent only.
